history.en: Ancient breed developed over 3,000 years ago in western Turkey for guarding livestock, particularly in the Akbash region. The white coat helped shepherds distinguish the dogs from predators. Introduced to North America in the 1970s for predator control programs.
history.zh: 古老的品种，3000多年前在土耳其西部培育用于护卫牲畜，特别是在阿克巴什地区。白色被毛帮助牧羊人将狗与捕食者区分开来。20世纪70年代引入北美用于捕食者控制项目。

overview.en: The Akbash is a large, powerful guardian breed from Western Turkey, developed over 3,000 years ago to protect livestock. They are independent, calm, and alert, with a strong protective instinct and deep loyalty to their family. Their white coat is not just for camouflage but also helps shepherds identify them among sheep. While they are not overly affectionate, they form strong bonds and are fearless when defending their territory. They are intelligent but can be stubborn, requiring experienced handling. Akbash dogs are generally reserved with strangers and need early socialization to prevent overprotectiveness. They thrive on having a job or purpose and are best suited for homes with space to roam.
overview.zh: 阿克巴什犬是一种大型、强壮的护卫犬种，源自土耳其西部，经过3000多年的培育用于保护牲畜。它们独立、冷静、警觉，具有很强的保护本能和对家庭的忠诚。白色被毛不仅用于伪装，还能帮助牧羊人在羊群中识别它们。虽然它们并不过分亲昵，但会形成深厚的情感纽带，并在保卫领地时无所畏惧。它们聪明但可能固执，需要经验丰富的饲养者。阿克巴什犬通常对陌生人保持警惕，需要早期社会化以防止过度保护。它们喜欢有工作或使命，最适合有空间活动的家庭。

care.en: Akbash dogs have a double coat that sheds heavily seasonally. Weekly brushing is needed, more often during shedding. They are clean dogs with minimal odor; bathe only when necessary. Exercise is moderate—they need daily walks or a large, securely fenced yard to patrol. They are not high-energy but require mental stimulation. Regular vet checkups are crucial for hip dysplasia and bloat, common in large breeds. Nail trimming, ear cleaning, and dental care should be part of the routine. Because of their guardian instinct, they may bark at night; provide a quiet sleeping area away from stimuli. Always supervise around small animals due to their prey drive.
care.zh: 阿克巴什犬有双层被毛，季节性大量脱毛。每周梳理一次，换毛期需要更频繁。它们干净，体味轻，仅在必要时洗澡。运动量适中——每天需要散步或一个大的、安全围栏的院子供其巡逻。它们精力不旺盛，但需要精神刺激。定期兽医检查对预防髋关节发育不良和胃扭转至关重要，这些在大型犬中常见。定期修剪指甲、清洁耳朵和牙齿护理应纳入日常。由于护卫本能，它们可能在夜间吠叫；提供远离刺激的安静睡眠区。由于猎物驱动力，始终监督它们与小动物相处。

training.en: Training an Akbash requires patience, consistency, and a calm but firm approach. They are independent thinkers and may question commands, so positive reinforcement with treats and praise works best. Harsh corrections can cause fear or aggression. Early socialization is essential to distinguish between normal and threatening situations. Obedience training should start young, focusing on recall and impulse control. Because they were bred to work independently, they excel in tasks that allow them to think, like scent work or guarding drills. Keep sessions short and engaging to hold their attention. Never use a retractable leash; a sturdy 6-foot leash is recommended for walks.
training.zh: 训练阿克巴什犬需要耐心、一致性和冷静而坚定的方法。它们是独立思考者，可能会质疑指令，因此使用零食和表扬的正面强化效果最好。严厉的纠正可能导致恐惧或攻击性。早期社会化至关重要，以区分正常和威胁性情况。服从训练应从幼年开始，重点训练召回和冲动控制。由于它们被培育成独立工作，它们在需要思考的任务中表现出色，如气味工作或护卫训练。保持训练简短有趣以吸引注意力。切勿使用伸缩牵引绳；建议使用结实的6英尺牵引绳。

diet.en: Feed a high-quality large-breed dog food with balanced protein and fat. Akbash dogs have a slower metabolism and can gain weight easily, so measure portions and avoid free-feeding. Divide daily food into two meals to reduce bloat risk. Include glucosamine and chondroitin supplements for joint health, especially as they age. Fresh water must always be available. Treats should be limited and not exceed 10% of daily calories. Some Akbash dogs have food sensitivities; monitor for skin or digestive issues. Puppies need a large-breed growth formula to control bone development. Consult your vet for specific dietary needs.
diet.zh: 喂养高品质的大型犬狗粮，蛋白质和脂肪均衡。阿克巴什犬新陈代谢较慢，容易发胖，因此要测量份量，避免自由采食。每日食物分成两餐以减少胃扭转风险。添加葡萄糖胺和软骨素补充剂以维护关节健康，特别是随着年龄增长。始终提供新鲜水。零食应限制，不超过每日热量的10%。一些阿克巴什犬有食物敏感性；注意皮肤或消化问题。幼犬需要大型犬成长配方以控制骨骼发育。咨询兽医了解具体饮食需求。

livingTips.en: Akbash dogs are not ideal for apartments due to their size and need for space. They thrive in rural or suburban homes with a large, secure yard. They are excellent watchdogs but may bark excessively if bored. They are generally good with children they are raised with, but their size can be intimidating; supervision is recommended. They may not get along with other dogs of the same sex and have a strong prey drive towards cats or small pets. Early introduction can help but never leave unsupervised. They prefer cooler climates and may struggle in heat; provide shade and water. A job like guarding livestock or property fulfills them.
livingTips.zh: 阿克巴什犬不适合公寓生活，因为它们体型大且需要空间。它们在乡村或郊区有大型安全院子的家庭中茁壮成长。它们是优秀的看门犬，但无聊时可能过度吠叫。通常对一起长大的孩子友善，但体型可能令人生畏；建议监督。它们可能不与同性别其他狗相处，并对猫或小宠物有强烈的猎物驱动力。早期介绍可能有帮助，但切勿无人看管。它们喜欢凉爽气候，可能在炎热天气中不适；提供阴凉和水。一份工作如守卫牲畜或财产能让它们满足。
